caveat: This score reflects a debug-mode smoke test on a 4-pair synthetic fixture with randomly-generated (not phangorn-heuristic) SPR labels -- NOT a genuine reproduction attempt on the paper's real Zenodo dataset. A score of 0.0 here means 'wrong dataset + 2 epochs', not 'broken implementation'. Re-run Stage 6 after real training on the downloaded dataset (python data/download.py) for a meaningful score.

root_causes.cause: Synthetic random-label toy dataset used instead of real Zenodo data
root_causes.suggested_fix: Run python data/download.py, then train.py without --debug or --data-dir data/toy

root_causes.cause: Only 2 training epochs (--debug) vs. early-stopping regime
root_causes.suggested_fix: Remove --debug; allow early stopping (patience 25) to govern training length

root_causes.cause: Test split of 1 pair -- not statistically meaningful
root_causes.suggested_fix: Use the real dataset's ~58-pair test split (15% of 388)

root_causes.cause: Synthetic random-label toy dataset used instead of real Zenodo data
root_causes.suggested_fix: Run python data/download.py, then train.py without --debug or --data-dir data/toy

root_causes.cause: Only 2 training epochs (--debug) vs. early-stopping regime
root_causes.suggested_fix: Remove --debug; allow early stopping (patience 25) to govern training length

root_causes.cause: R^2 is mathematically undefined on a 1-pair test split (needs >=2 unique target values); RegressionMetrics.compute() correctly returns NaN rather than a misleading number
root_causes.suggested_fix: Re-evaluate against the real dataset's full test split

root_causes.cause: Paper does not report an in-distribution single-split MAPE, only a stratified-CV aggregate MAPE (2.16% +/- 0.37%)
root_causes.suggested_fix: Compare against stratified_cv_overall MAPE once cross-validation is run, not the single-split in-distribution regime

parametric.evidence: SIR ambiguities[0], confidence 0.6: paper body text says 2 GIN layers, but Figure 5 diagrams 3 stacked GINConv/BatchNorm/ReLU blocks per branch.
parametric.suggested_fix: Config-swappable via model.num_gin_layers; run both 2 and 3 against real data and compare R^2 to Table 2 (0.873) to disambiguate.

parametric.evidence: Not stated anywhere in the paper (SIR implementation_assumptions[1], confidence 0.3).
parametric.suggested_fix: Tune against real training if convergence looks poor; unlikely to change final R^2 much at convergence, mainly affects training dynamics/speed.

parametric.evidence: Not stated; only early-stopping/LR-plateau patience windows are given (SIR implementation_assumptions[2], confidence 0.4).
parametric.suggested_fix: Bounded by early stopping regardless; increase if training has not converged by epoch 200 in practice.

parametric.evidence: Not stated; PyTorch nn.HuberLoss default used (SIR mathematical_spec[2], confidence 0.55). SPR distances range ~0-2000, so this default may be poorly scaled.
parametric.suggested_fix: If loss curves look unstable or the model underfits large-distance pairs, try scaling huber_delta up (e.g. 10-50) to better match the target range.

parametric.evidence: Not stated in the paper at all; PyTorch defaults used (SIR implementation_assumptions[0], confidence 0.55).
parametric.suggested_fix: Low expected impact; leave as-is unless training instability is observed.

parametric.evidence: Paper reports 'stratified cross-validation' results (mean +/- std) but never states the fold count.
parametric.suggested_fix: Adjust if a specific fold count is later confirmed from the authors' released code.

omission.missing_component: Full dataset-construction pre-processing pipeline (cgMLST profile slicing/stratification, controlled seeded shuffling, UPGMA/NJ tree inference via PhyloLib, midpoint re-rooting, phangorn/rspr SPR labelling)
omission.sir_location: Paper Sec 3, Fig. 1-2 (not part of sir.json's `architecture` -- that section only covers the GIN model, Sec 4)
omission.suggested_fix: This was a deliberate, documented scope decision (architecture_plan.json risk_assessment: 'Medium' severity, external Zenodo dataset) rather than an accidental omission -- this repo consumes the already-labelled released dataset rather than rebuilding it from raw cgMLST profiles. If full end-to-end pipeline reproduction (including dataset construction) is desired, that would need a separate R-based pre-processing module (PhyloLib, phangorn, rspr, Bio.Phylo) not currently implemented here.

user_config_modifications: --data-dir data/toy: synthetic 4-pair fixture with randomly-generated SPR labels used instead of the real Zenodo dataset (388 pairs)
review_reasons: User results come from a debug smoke-test on synthetic random-label data, not a real training run on the paper's actual dataset
R^2 undefined on test split (only 1 pair, single unique target value)
No genuine reproduction attempt against arXiv:2607.18311's real dataset has been performed yet -- this comparison exists to validate the Stage 6 reporting pipeline itself
